title: rubus rugosus subsp. rugosus
intro: Rubus rugosus subsp. rugosus, also known as the rough-leaved bramble, is a subspecies of the Rubus rugosus plant, a type of thorny shrub native to China. The leaves and roots of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus rugosus subsp. rugosus include reducing fever, relieving pain and swelling, and improving circulation, as well as its potential role in preventing cancer and cardiovascular disease. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional Chinese medicine.
